config.HeaderDescription: Configure OpenID Connect providers, authorization rules, and user provisioning behavior for Jellyfin SSO.
config.RestartNotice: Changes to this configuration require a Jellyfin restart.
config.UiCoverageNotice: This UI currently manages OpenID Connect providers. Some advanced options, including SAML provider setup, may still require the API.
config.SelfServiceNotice: Users can manage linked SSO accounts from the self-service linking page.
config.CustomMenuLinks: Jellyfin custom menu links can expose that page from the main menu.
config.ExistingProviderDescription: Select an OpenID provider to review, update, or remove its saved configuration.
config.ProviderConfigurationDescription: Create or update the OpenID Connect settings used for SSO sign-in and authorization.
config.DangerSettingsDescription: Options marked insecure weaken OIDC discovery or validation. Enable them only when a provider requires it and you understand the tradeoff.

sections.ProviderIdentityDescription: Name the provider and configure the OIDC endpoint and client credentials.
sections.AccessDefaultsDescription: Decide whether this provider is active and how default library access is assigned.
sections.RolesDescription: Match roles from OIDC claims to login, admin, and library permissions.
sections.LiveTvDescription: Control Live TV access through default settings or role-based rules.
sections.ClaimsDescription: Configure role claim parsing, requested scopes, username defaults, and avatar mapping.
sections.SecurityDescription: These options weaken OIDC discovery or validation. Use them only when required by a provider.

fields.ProviderNameDescription: The name used by Jellyfin to identify the OpenID provider. New names create providers; matching names update the existing provider.
fields.EndpointDescription: The OpenID endpoint. It must expose a .well-known discovery path.
fields.ClientIdDescription: The OpenID client ID configured on the provider to uniquely identify this Jellyfin instance.
fields.ClientSecretDescription: The OpenID client secret shared with the provider.
fields.EnableAuthorizationDescription: Determines if the plugin sets permissions for the user. If false, users start with no permissions and administrators assign them later. Existing user permissions are not rewritten on later logins.
fields.EnableAllFoldersDescription: If enabled, all libraries are accessible to users logging in through this provider.
fields.EnabledFoldersDescription: Determines which libraries are accessible to users logging in through this provider. This has no effect when Enable All Folders is checked.
fields.RolesDescription: Roles to look for in the OpenID response, one per line. Users with any listed role are authenticated. Leave blank to disable role checking.
fields.AdminRolesDescription: Admin roles to look for in the OpenID response, one per line. Users with any listed role receive administrator privileges.
fields.PreserveAdminPermissionsDescription: When enabled, the plugin can grant administrator access from matching admin roles but will not revoke administrator access from an account that already has it. Leave disabled to make OIDC the strict source of truth for admin status.
fields.EnableFolderRolesDescription: Determines if user roles control library access.
fields.FolderRoleMappingDescription: Map roles from the Role Claim to libraries. If a user has a mapped role, they receive access to the corresponding libraries.
fields.EnableLiveTvRolesDescription: Determines whether roles grant Live TV privileges.
fields.LiveTvRolesDescription: Roles to look for in the OpenID response, one per line. Users with any listed role receive Live TV access.
fields.LiveTvManagementRolesDescription: Roles to look for in the OpenID response, one per line. Users with any listed role receive Live TV management privileges.
fields.EnableLiveTvLabel: Enable Live TV Access By Default
fields.EnableLiveTvDescription: Determines whether users can view Live TV by default. This value is still used when Live TV RBAC is enabled.
fields.EnableLiveTvManagementLabel: Enable Live TV Management By Default
fields.EnableLiveTvManagementDescription: Determines whether users can manage Live TV by default. This value is still used when Live TV RBAC is enabled.
fields.RoleClaimDescription: The OpenID claim path used to read roles. Use \"\.\" for a literal dot. Examples: realm_access.roles, resource_access.<clientId>.roles, or groups.
fields.OidScopesDescription: Additional OIDC scopes to request, one per line. Leave blank to request only default scopes.
fields.DefaultProviderDescription: Authentication provider assigned after SSO login. Leave blank to keep the existing provider. A common default is Jellyfin.Server.Implementations.Users.DefaultAuthenticationProvider.
fields.DefaultUsernameClaimDescription: OpenID claim used as the Jellyfin username. Defaults to preferred_username when unset.
fields.AvatarUrlFormatDescription: Avatar URL format. OIDC claims can be referenced with @{claim_name}, for example https://example.invalid/@{user_id}.png.
fields.DisablePushedAuthorizationLabel: Disable Pushed Authorization (Insecure). May be needed for Authelia.
fields.DoNotValidateEndpointsDescription: May be required for Google OpenID.
fields.DoNotValidateIssuerNameLabel: Do Not Validate OpenID Issuer Name (Insecure)
fields.DoNotLoadProfileDescription: May be required for Cloudflare OpenID.
fields.SchemeOverrideDescription: If the plugin redirects to an insecure URL, set this to https.
fields.PortOverrideDescription: If the plugin redirects to an incorrect port, set this to the appropriate port.

messages.DeleteProviderConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the provider {0}?

linking.AccountLinkingDescription: Link this Jellyfin user to external OIDC or SAML identities, then remove stale links only after enabling delete mode.
linking.LinkProviderDescription: Use Link account on a provider card to add a new identity.
linking.RemoveLinkedIdentitiesDescription: Select linked identities above, enable delete mode, then delete the selected links.
linking.NoIdentityLinked: No identity linked to this provider.
